title: DNS over HTTPS & DNS over TLS
description: Encrypted DNS protocols that prevent eavesdropping and manipulation of DNS queries.
fullExplanation: Traditional DNS sends queries in plaintext UDP — anyone between you and the resolver can see every domain you visit. DoH (DNS over HTTPS) wraps DNS in HTTPS on port 443, making it indistinguishable from regular web traffic. DoT (DNS over TLS) wraps DNS in TLS on port 853. Both prevent ISPs, attackers, and network admins from seeing or modifying your DNS queries. The trade-off: you're trusting the DoH/DoT provider instead of your ISP.
keyFacts: DoH enabled by default in Firefox (uses Cloudflare) and Chrome
DoH bypasses corporate DNS filtering — security concern for enterprises
DoH3: DNS over HTTP/3 (QUIC) — even lower latency
Encrypted Client Hello (ECH): hides SNI in TLS — complements DoH for full privacy
Split-horizon DNS: DoH may bypass internal DNS — use canary domains to detect
Android 9+: Private DNS setting uses DoT. iOS 14+: supports DoH/DoT profiles.
